meaning: adj.刚孵出的,羽毛未丰的,矮胖的n.雏鸟,矮胖子adv.沉重地
examples: Both men said it takes a lot of food to raise squab.
In the smoking process, squab meat becomes very soft, almost like paste.
Bob Shipley says exporting smoked squab would not be a solution either.
The rich fruit flavor from the beer also pairs nicely with the glaze on the squab.
Between bites of coriander-scented lamb or squab with foie gras, take in the iconic Wrigley Building clock tower and Tribune Tower through the 30-foot floor-to-ceiling windows.
